title: osmundidae
intro: The osmundidae is a subfamily rank in plant taxonomy, specifically referring to a group of ferns within the family Osmundaceae. It is a division of ferns characterized by having a specific type of sporangium arrangement and often distinctive leaf morphology. Examples of orders within the subfamily Osmundidae include the Asparagales, which comprises plants such as lilies and asparagus, the Malpighiales, which includes plants like willows and mangroves, and the Poales, which includes plants like grasses and bromeliads. These orders, among others, are classified under the subfamily Osmundidae, highlighting the diversity of ferns within this taxonomic group.

orders.order_desc: Osmundales is an order of ferns within the class Polypodiopsida, division Pteridophyta, and kingdom Plantae.
